[00:01:11] Message from freetype2-2.13.2:
[00:01:11] 
[00:01:11] --
[00:01:11] The 2.7.x series now uses the new subpixel hinting mode (V40 port's option) as
[00:01:11] the default, emulating a modern version of ClearType. This change inevitably
[00:01:11] leads to different rendering results, and you might change port's options to
[00:01:11] adapt it to your taste (or use the new "FREETYPE_PROPERTIES" environment
[00:01:11] variable).
[00:01:11] 
[00:01:11] The environment variable "FREETYPE_PROPERTIES" can be used to control the
[00:01:11] driver properties. Example:
[00:01:11] 
[00:01:11] FREETYPE_PROPERTIES=truetype:interpreter-version=35 \
[00:01:11] 	cff:no-stem-darkening=1 \
[00:01:11] 	autofitter:warping=1
[00:01:11] 
[00:01:11] This allows to select, say, the subpixel hinting mode at runtime for a given
[00:01:11] application.
[00:01:11] 
[00:01:11] If LONG_PCF_NAMES port's option was enabled, the PCF family names may include
[00:01:11] the foundry and information whether they contain wide characters. For example,
[00:01:11] "Sony Fixed" or "Misc Fixed Wide", instead of "Fixed". This can be disabled at
[00:01:11] run time with using pcf:no-long-family-names property, if needed. Example:
[00:01:11] 
[00:01:11] FREETYPE_PROPERTIES=pcf:no-long-family-names=1
[00:01:11] 
[00:01:11] How to recreate fontconfig cache with using such environment variable,
[00:01:11] if needed:
[00:01:11] # env FREETYPE_PROPERTIES=pcf:no-long-family-names=1 fc-cache -fsv
[00:01:11] 
[00:01:11] The controllable properties are listed in the section "Controlling FreeType
[00:01:11] Modules" in the reference's table of contents
[00:01:11] (/usr/local/share/doc/freetype2/reference/index.html, if documentation was installed).

[00:01:11] --
[00:01:11] Make sure that the freetype module is loaded.  If it is not, add the following
[00:01:11] line to the "Modules" section of your X Windows configuration file:
[00:01:11] 
[00:01:11] 	Load "freetype"
[00:01:11] 
[00:01:11] Add the following line to the "Files" section of X Windows configuration file:
[00:01:11] 
[00:01:11] 	FontPath "/usr/local/share/fonts/dejavu/"
[00:01:11] 
[00:01:11] Note: your X Windows configuration file is typically /etc/X11/XF86Config
[00:01:11] if you are using XFree86, and /etc/X11/xorg.conf if you are using X.Org.

[00:01:11] Message from openjdk17-17.0.9+9.1:
[00:01:11] 
[00:01:11] --
[00:01:11] This OpenJDK implementation requires fdescfs(5) mounted on /dev/fd and
[00:01:11] procfs(5) mounted on /proc.
[00:01:11] 
[00:01:11] If you have not done it yet, please do the following:
[00:01:11] 
[00:01:11] 	mount -t fdescfs fdesc /dev/fd
[00:01:11] 	mount -t procfs proc /proc
[00:01:11] 
[00:01:11] To make it permanent, you need the following lines in /etc/fstab:
[00:01:11] 
[00:01:11] 	fdesc	/dev/fd		fdescfs		rw	0	0
[00:01:11] 	proc	/proc		procfs		rw	0	0
